framework.name: Hourly Base Rate to Project Pricing Formula
framework.description: Start with uncomfortable hourly rate. Calculate expected project hours × hourly rate. Mark up 50-200% for margin and error buffer. Prevents underpricing while maintaining connection to time-value baseline. Markup captures efficiency gains and scope uncertainty.
framework.raw_quote: take how many hours you expect that project to be, multiply it by the hourly. So say your hourly is $50. You think the project will take let's say 100 hours. So that would be $5,000 and then mark it up by 50 to 200%.

source.title: $50/Hour to $10K Agents: AI Voice Agency Pricing Masterclass
